strengths: Clearly identifies three specific, real-world objects and situates each in its authentic context
Explicit and relevant links made between each object and the chosen IA prompt
Defensible justifications for object selection that advance the overall argument
Most claims supported by appropriate external evidence and explicit references to TOK concepts
weaknesses: Occasional lapses into descriptive rather than analytical commentary
Final synthesis paragraph remains cursory and underdeveloped
Key TOK concepts (e.g., Ways of Knowing) are only implicitly addressed
Minor citation inconsistencies and some repetition detract from clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission meets the descriptors for band 7â8 by clearly identifying three real-world objects and providing well-explained links and justifications tied to the IA prompt. Evidence is appropriately used to support claims, and TOK concepts are sustained throughout. However, occasional descriptive passages, a cursory synthesis, and minor technical issues prevent it from reaching the 9â10 level. An 8 out of 10 reflects this very strong performance with minor areas for improvement.

annotations.comment: The paragraph ends abruptly with âdue to aâ, indicating a sentence fragment. Complete this thought to maintain coherence.

The student clearly states the chosen prompt, which is a strength. To deepen engagement, consider defining key TOK terms (e.g., knowledge, dissemination) up front to establish a clear conceptual framework.
The introduction offers a thesis but would benefit from explicit TOK terminology (for example, referencing specific Ways of Knowing) to clarify how later analysis will address the prompt.
Object 1 is clearly identified as a specific, real-world artefact with context (The Matcha Tokyo), satisfying the requirement to situate your examples authentically.
The image and alt text are detailed, which is good for accessibility. However, the alt text duplicates the description; streamline to avoid redundancy.
The description of Object 1 is heavily descriptive. To align more closely with the prompt, integrate explicit analysis of how commercial hype reflects challenges in the communication of knowledge.
Use of external sources (Mayo Clinic, Han 2024) strengthens your justification. Ensure citation formats are consistent (e.g., author-year style) throughout.
The header for Object 2 is clear, but adding details such as the videoâs release date and platform would better establish its real-world context.
The still image is illustrative, yet the visible âBLACKPINKâ signage may distract from the intended TWICE-focused example. Crop or annotate to maintain relevance.
While the argument is coherent, delve deeper into how the algorithmic design of social media platforms amplifies certain voices to unpack the TOK implications fully.
The conclusion touches on societal impact but remains cursory. Strengthen synthesis by explicitly invoking TOK frameworks (e.g., Ways of Knowing, Areas of Knowledge).
The final sentence is too general. Reinforce your closure by directly restating how each object collectively answers the prompt question.
Reference formatting is inconsistent. Ensure every entry uses the same citation style, including italicization, punctuation, and retrieval dates.
The Mullinix (2024) reference lacks a full title and URL. Complete this entry to allow readers to locate the source readily.
This description focuses on the anecdote but lacks an explicit link back to the IA prompt. Elaborate on how cultural misinterpretation challenges the communication of knowledge.
Your cultural analysis is insightful, yet it would be strengthened by naming specific Ways of Knowing (e.g., language, emotion) to ground the discussion in TOK theory.
Object 3 is precisely identified with its academic source, satisfying the requirement for a specific, real-world artefact and context.
The network diagram is compelling, but the write-up should critique the studyâs methodologyâconsider how algorithmic biases shape the visualization and influence dissemination.
The analysis effectively links authority (Trump) and technology to illustrate how power dynamics exacerbate misinformation, directly addressing the prompt.
The Peck (2024) citation should follow the same format as other entries (e.g., italicization of article title, date placement). Review all references for consistency.
The introduction could preview the three objects to improve structural coherence and guide the reader through your exhibition plan.
Your discussion of skepticism touches on reason vs. emotion but does not name these Ways of Knowing. Explicitly connect your example to TOK concepts for deeper analysis.
Provide more detail about the publication (journal name, date) of the Scientific Reports article to flesh out the real-world context of Object 3.
This section repeats sentence openings like âThis object also presents...â. Vary phrasing to maintain reader engagement and reduce redundancy.
Ensure that hyperlinks in your references are live and correctly formatted (e.g., remove angle brackets) to improve accessibility.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

The exhibition presents
three objects, but the
real-world contexts of
these objects are not
stated, or the images
presented may be
highly generic images of
types of object rather
than of specific
real-world objects. Links
between the objects
and the selected IA
prompt are made, but
these are minimal,
tenuous, or it is not
clear what the student is
trying to convey.
There is very little
justification offered for
the inclusion of each
object in the exhibition.
The commentary on the
objects is highly
descriptive or consists
only of unsupported
assertions.

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.

rubricCriterionStrands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
